explanation.rule: These suffixes transform nouns and verbs into adjectives that describe qualities or characteristics.

groups.note: Forms adjectives meaning 'having a tendency to' or 'relating to'

examples.definition: wanting strongly to win or be the best

groups.note: Forms adjectives meaning 'can be done' or 'worthy of'

examples.definition: able to change to suit new conditions

groups.note: Forms adjectives meaning 'full of' or 'having the quality of'

wordForms.definition: wanting strongly to win or be the most successful
wordForms.example: The market has become extremely competitive in recent years.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to try to win or be better than others

wordForms.definition: successful in producing the intended result
wordForms.example: Exercise is an effective way to reduce stress.
wordForms.rootDefinition: the result or impact of an action

wordForms.definition: evoking admiration through size or quality
wordForms.example: The team delivered an impressive performance.

wordForms.definition: good at convincing others to do or believe something
wordForms.example: Her argument was so persuasive that everyone agreed.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to cause someone to believe or do something

wordForms.example: His commitment to the community is truly admirable.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to respect and approve of someone

wordForms.definition: able to change easily to suit new conditions
wordForms.example: You need to be adaptable to succeed in a changing workplace.

wordForms.definition: large or important enough to be noticed
wordForms.example: The project required a considerable amount of time and effort.
The city is famous for its beautiful architecture.
wordForms.rootDefinition: the state of being well known

wordForms.definition: strange and not easy to explain
wordForms.example: A mysterious package arrived at the office with no name on it.

wordForms.definition: very important, especially in its future effects
wordForms.example: The signing of the agreement was a momentous occasion.

wordForms.definition: of different types or many different
wordForms.example: The report was written for various reasons.
wordForms.rootDefinition: to be different or to change

wordForms.example: She was nervous about presenting in front of the whole school.
wordForms.rootDefinition: relating to the body's nerve system; apprehensive

multipleChoiceExercises.sentence: The new marketing strategy proved highly ___ in boosting sales.
She gave such a ___ speech that the committee changed their minds.
The athletes train hard to stay ___ at the international level.
His dedication to helping others is truly ___.
The town is ___ for its annual film festival.
Strange footprints in the snow remained ___.

transformExercises.sentence: The price of the flat has dropped by a ___ amount. (CONSIDER)
He proved to be a ___ employee who learned every system quickly. (ADAPT)
The view from the hilltop was absolutely ___. (IMPRESS)
Winning the championship was a ___ moment for the whole town. (MOMENT)
She gave a range of ___ answers to choose from. (VARY)
He felt ___ before every job interview, no matter how prepared he was. (NERVE)
